product_summary.one_sentence_summary: Turnkey language-and-orientation package that staffing agencies can bundle into cross-border placements for workers moving into Benelux roles.
product_summary.target_user: EU staffing and relocation agencies placing workers in Belgium, Luxembourg and nearby Benelux markets.
product_summary.core_pain: Agencies need a simple way to raise placement value while reducing onboarding friction for candidates moving across borders.
product_summary.claimed_outcome: A bundled language and orientation package that increases the value of a placement and helps workers settle faster.

primary_buyer_type.reason: The source card is explicitly written for staffing and relocation agencies.

secondary_buyer_types.reason: Employers that receive placed workers may co-fund or co-own the onboarding motion.
The model likely needs local training or relocation partners to be credible.

budget_owner.reason: For staffing agencies, this would usually sit with a commercial or operations business unit rather than a general L&D owner.

buyer_influencers.reason: This is a partner-heavy cross-border motion and may require founder-level approval in smaller agencies.
Cross-border work eligibility, permit and localisation questions influence whether the offer is viable.
Bundle pricing and placement margin assumptions need commercial review.
Employer-side onboarding stakeholders can shape the usefulness of the package.

externally_checkable_claims.claim_text: A turnkey language package increases an agency's value proposition and fee.
externally_checkable_claims.notes: This is a commercial hypothesis; no public source reviewed validates the price uplift for this exact offer.
No public source reviewed confirmed this market-growth figure for the relevant segment.
The reviewed public sources show EU Blue Card rule changes and Luxembourg issuance counts, but not this exact growth statistic.

externally_checkable_claims.claim_text: The revised EU Blue Card framework lowers thresholds and allows a 6-month contract.
externally_checkable_claims.notes: European Commission materials on the revised Blue Card framework state that the contract duration requirement is six months and that Luxembourg applies the revised threshold.

externally_checkable_claims.claim_text: More workers need integration because Blue Card rules are looser.
externally_checkable_claims.notes: The Blue Card framework is indeed more flexible, but the link to a broad increase in agency demand is an inference, not a directly verified market result.

externally_checkable_claims.claim_text: A turnkey package can be bundled into the agency placement fee.
externally_checkable_claims.notes: This is a business-model assumption and was not externally verified for this specific product.

internal_claims_unverified.claim_text: Agencies can charge €1,500 more per placement with language included.
internal_claims_unverified.reason_unverified: No public source reviewed confirmed this pricing uplift.
This is an internal projection, not an externally verified market fact.

internal_claims_unverified.claim_text: Identify 2–3 staffing agencies in Luxembourg and Belgium and convert them into pilots.
internal_claims_unverified.reason_unverified: This is an execution plan, not a public fact.

regulator_entities.name: European Commission, Directorate-General for Migration and Home Affairs
regulator_entities.relevance: Runs the EU Blue Card portal and publishes the framework used for highly qualified cross-border work mobility.

regulator_entities.name: General Department of Immigration, Ministry of Home Affairs (Luxembourg)
regulator_entities.relevance: The Luxembourg Blue Card page states that applications go through the Ministry of Home Affairs.

framework_entities.name: Directive (EU) 2021/1883 (Recast Blue Card Directive)
framework_entities.relevance: The EU Blue Card framework now uses a 6-month contract threshold and a simplified mobility regime in Member States.

regulatory_requirements.requirement: For the EU Blue Card in Luxembourg, the applicant must have a 6-month contract and meet the salary threshold of EUR 58,968.
regulatory_requirements.applies_to: Highly qualified third-country workers applying in Luxembourg

regulatory_requirements.requirement: Only EU Member States can issue EU Blue Cards and the regime applies in 25 of the 27 EU Member States.
regulatory_requirements.applies_to: Highly qualified third-country workers and sponsoring employers

regulatory_requirements.requirement: For regulated professions under the Blue Card framework, national legal requirements must be met.

competitors_or_substitutes.name: LiReco
competitors_or_substitutes.notes: Integrated recruitment, language training and relocation model for healthcare professionals moving across Europe.

competitors_or_substitutes.name: Charité international recruitment of nurses
competitors_or_substitutes.notes: Employer-led international recruitment with language and integration support.

competitors_or_substitutes.name: HSE international recruitment relocation package
competitors_or_substitutes.notes: Public employer relocation package that pays some visa, registration and language-testing costs for incoming staff.

legal_claim_severity.reason: The product is commercial onboarding support, but it touches immigration and lawful work-routing claims.

authority_needed_for_delivery.reason: A credible delivery likely needs country-specific legal and relocation expertise, though not a formal accreditation route.

authority_needed_for_claims.reason: Claims about lawful work routes and Blue Card eligibility should be supported by external expertise or legal review.

proof_stack.reason: Staffing agencies need to believe the package will improve placement economics and reduce friction.
A paid agency pilot is the first meaningful proof that the bundle has commercial pull.
The package only matters if it helps produce successful placements and handoffs.

proof_burden.reason: The model needs measurable buyer acceptance and real placement outcomes, but not regulated recognition proof.

dependency_types.reason: The offer depends on recruitment or relocation agencies as the channel of delivery.
Benelux rules and expectations differ enough that local adaptation is required.
Immigration and onboarding claims need external specialist input to stay credible.
The offer depends on the state accepting the applicant's legal route to work.

scope_variance_risk.reason: Even within Benelux, work-eligibility and onboarding routes vary enough to affect execution.

expansion_variance_risk.reason: Expansion beyond the current countries would add more permit, language and localisation variance.

subsidy_exists.reason: No clear subsidy or co-funding route is being relied on for this product.

subsidy_eligibility_status.reason: The product card does not depend on a subsidy or reimbursement mechanism.

subsidy_conflict_flag.reason: No meaningful funding claim is made.

market_regulatory_context.reason: The product sits next to immigration and work-permit rules rather than formal professional accreditation.

claim_language_risk.reason: Commercial language about increasing fees and smoothing cross-border work can become overstated quickly.

authority_dependency_risk.reason: Work eligibility depends on national legal routes and Blue Card acceptance, which the provider does not control.

competitive_gap_risk.reason: Integrated recruitment plus language plus relocation models already exist, so uniqueness claims are weak.

numeric_claim_risk.reason: The RPO CAGR, price uplift and Blue Card growth figures are not verified in the reviewed sources.

subsidy_conflict_risk.reason: The card does not rely on a subsidy story.

positioning_risk.reason: This is closer to a cross-border service operation than a simple training product.

claim_conflicts.what_is_wrong: No public source reviewed confirmed this market-growth figure for the relevant segment.
The reviewed Blue Card sources describe rule changes and Luxembourg thresholds, but not this exact growth statistic.

claim_conflicts.claim_text: Agencies charge €1,500 more per placement with language included.
claim_conflicts.what_is_wrong: No public source reviewed confirmed this pricing uplift.

recommended_manual_review.reason: Decide whether the offer is a fee uplift add-on, a bundle inside the placement fee, or a separate service line.
Validate whether the initial scope is actually Benelux or only Belgium-Luxembourg with Netherlands as a future expansion.
